_doc: Web-search collection line. Separate from structured tools (pivot_map/tools_registry). Each searchable selector type declares a profile that GUIDES the web-search collector skill: a strategy, seed query templates (the collector also generates adaptive follow-ups), which result domains are worth fetching, and which entity types to extract. 'priority' = primary (web search is the main line for this type) or supplemental (structured tools lead; web search fills gaps). The collector returns RAW cited findings; the supervisor tiers and commits them.

name.strategy: Identify and disambiguate the real person. Highest value: employer + role/title, education, professional licenses (attorney/medical/etc.), location, contact info (email/phone), and family/associates. Public records, LinkedIn, .edu/.gov, and licensing/directory pages carry the most signal. For common names, only attach a finding if a corroborating detail ties it to the SAME individual; flag ambiguous matches.
query_templates: "{selector}" (attorney OR lawyer OR professor OR director OR engineer OR founder)
"{selector}" (university OR college OR "law school" OR PhD OR JD OR MBA)
"{selector}" (address OR phone OR relatives OR "lives in")
"{selector}" (clustrmaps OR spokeo OR radaris OR fastpeoplesearch OR thatsthem)
name.snippet_note: People-search aggregators (clustrmaps/spokeo/radaris/...) BLOCK direct fetch but expose co-residents/DOB/phone IN THE SEARCH SNIPPET. Read the snippet; co-residents at one address = the family network. Don't give up on a 403.

username.strategy: The structured line (sherlock/maigret) enumerates account existence; web search is for ATTRIBUTION and content. Fetch the real profile pages to pull display name, bio, linked emails/sites, and cross-platform handles. Confirm a profile actually exists and belongs to the subject (vs a namespace collision).

email.strategy: Tie the address to an identity and surface exposure. Look for the owner's name, associated usernames/profiles, and any breach/paste/leak mentions. The local-part often reveals a username to pivot on.
query_templates: "{selector}" (password OR leak OR breach OR dump)

company.strategy: Map the organization: official site/domains, key people, employees, locations, registrations. LinkedIn and business registries are highest value.

phone.strategy: Attribute the number to a person/business via reverse-lookup and social mentions. Name + location are the prize.

domain.strategy: Structured tools (whois/dns/crt.sh) lead. Use web search for exposed files, leaked secrets, and who is behind it.
query_templates: site:{selector} filetype:pdf OR filetype:xlsx OR filetype:csv

telegram_handle.strategy: telegram_handle has no structured tools, so web search IS the line. Find the channel/user, real name, linked handles on other platforms, and any indexing/mirror sites.
